judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports nail the default-behavior findings, but Report 2 additionally and clearly recommends a global env-level gate ANDed with the per-tenant flag, making it the stronger answer overall.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

report_2.evidence: Reminders will be sent automatically. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled(tenant)` at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

report_1.evidence: I would change the return value at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` from `return True` to `return False` (mirroring the existing pattern but inverting the default)

report_2.evidence: I would change the default at `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`, inverting it so a missing/None value returns `False` (i.e., delete the `is None -> True` special case entirely, or change it to return `False`)

report_1.evidence: the default was inherited by structural copy-paste from an unrelated toggle with a very different risk profile (visual preference vs. an outbound patient-communication feature), with no test, no seed-data update

report_2.evidence: added by literally duplicating the structure of `compact_calendar_view_enabled`, which was introduced one commit earlier (`25509a7`, SLOT-190) for a calendar display preference — a pure UI/authoring convenience with essentially zero blast radius if wrongly defaulted on

Add an env-level master switch that is ANDed with the per-tenant flag before the job actually sends (per project documentation's flag recipe, step 3), so there's a single global kill switch to stop all outbound reminder sends without having to touch every tenant's settings — I found no such switch anywhere in the repo (`infra/main.tf` only defines an `environment` variable).
